content:
Evaluation of Autonomous UAV Surveillance Systems
Abstract
This report evaluates autonomous UAV surveillance systems deployed for border security. The system achieved an accuracy of 98.4% during all experiments. However, another evaluation later reports the accuracy as 91.2% under the same testing conditions.
Introduction
Autonomous drones are increasingly used for surveillance and reconnaissance missions. They improve monitoring capabilities and reduce human intervention.

improve monitoring capabilities and reduce human intervention.
The proposed system is suitable for all environmental conditions.
System Overview
The surveillance platform uses RGB cameras, thermal sensors, GPS navigation, and Edge AI processing.
The system always detects every intrusion successfully.
Experimental Results
The evaluation was performed using 150 surveillance missions.
Later in this report, the experiment is described as containing 120 surveillance missions.

xperiment is described as containing 120 surveillance missions.
Average detection accuracy: 98.4% with a false alarm rate of 1.2%.
Another section states a detection accuracy of 91.2% with a false alarm rate of 4.8%.
Performance
The processing latency was approximately 50 milliseconds.
Later, the report states the same processing latency is 350 milliseconds without explaining the difference.
Security Analysis
The encryption algorithm is highly secure.

e.
Security Analysis
The encryption algorithm is highly secure.
No security evaluation, benchmark, or comparison is provided.
The communication protocol is extremely reliable.
No experimental evidence is presented.
Deployment
The system performs well in adverse weather.
The system may fail during heavy rainfall.
Conclusion
The proposed system completely eliminates all security risks.
The report does not provide any experimental evidence supporting this conclusion.
